heading: Lighting, heating, and ventilating House of Representatives
label: 1833 Lighting, heating, and ventilating House of Representatives
text: The electrician, together with everything pertaining to the electrical machinery and apparatus, and the ventilation and heating of the House of Representatives, and all laborers and others connected with the lighting, heating, and ventilating thereof, shall be subject exclusively to the orders, and in all respects under the direction, of the Architect of the Capitol, subject to the control of the Speaker; and no removal or appointment shall be made except with his approval. And all engineers and others who are engaged in heating and ventilating the House shall be subject to the orders, and in all respects under the direction, of the Architect of the Capitol, subject to the control of the Speaker; and no removal or appointment shall be made except with his approval.
notes: CodificationSection was classified to section 167 of former Title 40, prior to the enactment of Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, by , , .Pub. L. 107–217, § 1116 Stat. 1062Aug. 21, 2002Section, except the words “and the ventilation and heating of the House of Representatives,” is based on act , popularly known as the “Legislative, Executive, and Judicial Appropriation Act”. The excepted words were based on act , popularly known as the “Sundry Civil Appropriation Act, fiscal year 1878”.Mar. 3, 1881Mar. 3, 1877
